admissions.course_rigor: All of our programs reflect the University’s commitment to experiential education and interdisciplinary coursework and are taught by renowned faculty composed of leaders and visionaries within their respective fields. Drexel has a wide variety of majors and minors that will allow you to explore your individual interests and make an impact in the world around you.

projects_research: Research & Innovation Research & Innovation Institutes & Initiatives Innovation & Entrepreneurship Industry Engagement Global Partnerships

evidence.source_excerpt: A Drexel education will encourage you to explore your interests and allow you to develop your own path. With over 100 majors and as many minors, you're sure to find a program that speaks to you. Through our cross-disciplinary model of learning, you'll also have the opportunity to explore a diverse range of minors outside of your chosen major, allowing you to expand the breadth and depth of your knowledge and get the most from your education. No matter what you choose to study, your classes will be taught by accomplished faculty, many of whom are working professionals teaching in their area of expertise.

popular_professors.ranking_basis: RateMyProfessors entries sorted by rating_count desc, then average_rating desc, then would_take_again_percent desc.
popular_professors.source_summary: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Drexel University' (legacyId=1521) and ranked all professors with at least one rating.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Curated from official Drexel University College of Medicine Alumni Magazine stories on drexel.edu, prioritizing person-specific alumni features with explicit class-year evidence in page titles and substantive career impact described in official article metadata or page copy.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Extracted from official Drexel University College of Medicine Alumni Magazine archive pages on drexel.edu.

items.name: Ana Núñez
items.bio: Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine says Ana Núñez, MD, HU ’86, ELAM ’05 became an academic-medicine leader whose work at Drexel and later the University of Minnesota focused on medical education and diversity, equity, and inclusion.
items.description: Physician-educator and academic-medicine leader in medical education and DEI.
items.confirmation_label: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine page with explicit HU ’86 and ELAM ’05 class-year evidence in the title and substantive official profile text in the article metadata and body.

items.name: Asif Ilyas
items.bio: Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine says Asif Ilyas, MD, MCPHU ’01, MBA, FACS, serves as associate dean of clinical research, is a professor of orthopedic surgery at Thomas Jefferson University, and has published more than 200 peer-reviewed studies and papers.
items.description: Orthopedic surgeon, professor, and prolific clinical-research leader.
items.confirmation_label: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine Q&A page with explicit MCPHU ’01 class-year evidence in the title and a strong official career summary in the metadata.

items.name: Loretta Christensen
items.bio: Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine identifies Loretta Christensen, MD, HU ’84, as chief medical officer of the Indian Health Service.
items.description: Chief medical officer of the Indian Health Service.
items.confirmation_label: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine interview page with explicit HU ’84 class-year evidence in the title and an official role summary in the metadata.

items.name: Richard Hamilton
items.bio: Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine says Richard Hamilton, MD, HU ’87, became the academic chair of Drexel’s Department of Emergency Medicine after building a multifaceted career as an emergency-medicine clinician-educator and scholar.
items.confirmation_label: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine profile with explicit HU ’87 class-year evidence in the title and substantive official career details in the metadata and body.

items.name: Jennifer (Rizzo) Myers
items.bio: Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine says Jennifer (Rizzo) Myers, MD, MCPHU ’98, stayed in academic medicine, became professor of clinical medicine at Penn, and showed an early ability to anticipate where medical science was heading.
items.description: Academic physician and professor of clinical medicine.
items.confirmation_label: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine profile with explicit MCPHU ’98 class-year evidence in the title and substantive official career details in the metadata and body.

items.name: Romaine Johnson
items.bio: Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine says Romaine Johnson, MD, MCPHU ’99, pursued research and academic medicine because she saw that research could improve population outcomes while also changing individual lives.
items.description: Academic physician-researcher focused on population and patient impact.
items.confirmation_label: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine profile with explicit MCPHU ’99 class-year evidence in the title and substantive official profile text in the metadata and body.

items.name: Helen Morrison
items.bio: Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine says Helen Morrison, MD, MCP ’73, built a 50-year career as a criminal forensic psychiatrist, writing books and articles and profiling serial killers around the world.
items.confirmation_label: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine feature with explicit MCP ’73 class-year evidence in the article text and a substantive official career summary in the metadata.

items.name: Tina Adjei
items.bio: Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine says Tina Adjei, MD, MCPHU ’99, has worked across emergency medicine, utilization review, and physician mentoring while trying to reduce racial and socioeconomic inequality in care delivery.
items.description: Emergency physician and health-equity-oriented care leader.
items.confirmation_label: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine feature with explicit MCPHU ’99 class-year evidence in the title and a substantive official career/impact summary in the metadata.

items.name: J. Kenneth Brubaker
items.bio: Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine says J. Kenneth Brubaker, MD, HU ’70, returned to underserved Mount Joy after graduation, built a family medicine practice from three to 10 health practitioners, and devoted his career to caring for elderly and underserved patients.
items.description: Family physician who expanded care in an underserved community.
items.confirmation_label: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine feature with explicit HU ’70 class-year evidence in the title and a substantive official community-impact summary in the metadata.

items.name: Jeannette A. Chirico-Post
items.bio: Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine says Jeannette A. Chirico-Post, MD, WMC ’68, graduated determined to support women pursuing medicine while advancing her own career despite discrimination faced by female physicians of her era.
items.description: Physician advocate for women in medicine and veterans.
items.confirmation_label: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine profile
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Drexel College of Medicine Alumni Magazine feature with explicit WMC ’68 class-year evidence in the title and a substantive official profile summary in the metadata.
